makers.title: Anthropic
makers.blurb: Anthropic builds the Claude family of large language models, organized into capability tiers — Opus (flagship), Sonnet (balanced), Haiku (fast and economical) — plus a newer top-end "Mythos-class" tier (Fable 5 / Mythos 5). Anthropic does not ship open-weight LLMs.

lines.title: Claude Fable / Mythos (Mythos-class)
lines.blurb: Anthropic's most capable, top-end tier — a higher intelligence class than Opus, released publicly as Fable 5 with safety classifiers and to restricted partners as Mythos 5.

versions.name: Claude Fable 5
versions.blurb: First widely-released Mythos-class model; 1M context, 128K max output, $10/$50 per MTok. Always-on thinking; ships with safety classifiers that fall…

versions.name: Claude Mythos 5
versions.blurb: Same underlying model as Fable 5 with some safeguards lifted; available only through Project Glasswing (cyberdefense/infrastructure partners, with th…

versions.name: Claude Mythos Preview
versions.blurb: Invitation-only early Mythos-class model used by a small number of organizations for cybersecurity work; superseded by Mythos 5.

lines.title: Claude Opus
lines.blurb: Anthropic's flagship Claude tier — most capable Opus-class model for the hardest reasoning, coding, and long-horizon agentic work.

versions.name: Claude Opus 4.8
versions.blurb: Opus-tier flagship (May 2026). Better coding/knowledge work and tool-calling than 4.7; ~4x less likely to let its own code flaws pass unremarked. 1M context.

versions.name: Claude Opus 4.7
versions.blurb: Stronger software engineering and long-running coding; higher-resolution vision (~3.3x); new xhigh effort level. Removed sampling params and budget_t…

versions.name: Claude Opus 4.6
versions.blurb: First Opus-class model with a 1M token context window (beta at launch). Improved planning, larger-codebase reliability, code review and debugging; ad…

versions.name: Claude Opus 4.5
versions.blurb: Completed the Claude 4.5 family; positioned as Anthropic's most intelligent model at launch with a roughly two-thirds price cut versus Opus 4.1. Adde…

versions.name: Claude Opus 4.1
versions.blurb: Incremental upgrade to Opus 4 with improved coding and agentic performance.

versions.name: Claude Opus 4
versions.blurb: First Claude 4-generation flagship, launched alongside Sonnet 4 with hybrid reasoning and extended thinking.

versions.name: Claude 3 Opus
versions.blurb: Top-tier model of the original Claude 3 family; most capable Claude at its launch.

lines.title: Claude Sonnet
lines.blurb: Anthropic's balanced mid-tier — the best mix of speed and intelligence for everyday and production workloads.

versions.name: Claude Sonnet 5
versions.blurb: Sonnet tier (Jun 2026); more agentic Sonnet with autonomous planning, browser/terminal tool use, and lower cost than Opus 4.8. 1M context, 128K max output, $3/$15 per MTok ($2/$10 intro through Aug 31, 2026).

versions.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
versions.blurb: Sonnet tier (Feb 2026); best speed/intelligence balance. 1M context, 64K max output, $3/$15 per MTok. Adaptive thinking; effort parameter.

versions.name: Claude Sonnet 4.5
versions.blurb: Opened the Claude 4.5 family; strong agentic and coding performance for a mid-tier model.

versions.name: Claude Sonnet 4
versions.blurb: Claude 4-generation Sonnet, launched with Opus 4; hybrid reasoning with extended thinking.

versions.name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
versions.blurb: First Claude model with extended/visible thinking; bridged the 3.5 and 4 generations. Retired 2026-02-19.

versions.name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet (new)
versions.blurb: Upgraded 3.5 Sonnet ('new'/v2); introduced computer use in beta. Retired 2025-10-28.

versions.name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
versions.blurb: First 3.5-generation model; outperformed Claude 3 Opus at lower cost. Retired 2025-10-28.

versions.name: Claude 3 Sonnet
versions.blurb: Mid-tier of the original Claude 3 family. Retired 2025-07-21.

lines.title: Claude Haiku
lines.blurb: Anthropic's fastest and most cost-effective Claude tier, for high-volume and latency-sensitive tasks.

versions.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
versions.blurb: Haiku tier (Oct 2025); fastest and most economical Claude. 200K context, 64K max output, $1/$5 per MTok. Supports adaptive thinking.

versions.name: Claude 3.5 Haiku
versions.blurb: 3.5-generation small model; faster and cheaper than 3.5 Sonnet. Retired 2026-02-19.

versions.name: Claude 3 Haiku
versions.blurb: Fastest model of the original Claude 3 family; deprecated, retiring 2026-04-19.

lines.title: Claude (legacy 1–2.x)
lines.blurb: Anthropic's pre-Claude-3 models, including the original Claude and the low-latency Claude Instant; all retired.

versions.name: Claude 2.1
versions.blurb: Added a 200K context window and reduced hallucination rates over Claude 2. Retired 2025-07-21.

versions.name: Claude 2
versions.blurb: Second-generation Claude with a 100K context window and improved coding/reasoning. Retired 2025-07-21.

versions.name: Claude Instant
versions.blurb: Original low-latency, lower-cost Claude variant, launched alongside Claude 1.

versions.name: Claude 1
versions.blurb: Anthropic's first publicly released Claude model.

makers.title: OpenAI
makers.blurb: Creator of ChatGPT and the GPT model family, spanning proprietary flagship, reasoning, fast, small, and coding lines plus the open-weight gpt-oss models.

lines.title: GPT (Flagship / Thinking)
lines.blurb: OpenAI's flagship reasoning model line for complex agentic coding, tool use, and professional work.

versions.name: GPT-5.6 Sol
versions.blurb: GPT-5.6 flagship, GA July 9, 2026. Adds max reasoning effort and ultra subagent mode; 1.05M context; 88.8% on Terminal-Bench 2.1; $5/$30 per MTok.

versions.name: GPT-5.6 Terra
versions.blurb: GPT-5.6 balanced mid-tier, GA July 9, 2026. Positioned as competitive with GPT-5.5 at 2x lower cost; 1.05M context; 84.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.1; $2.50/$15 per MTok.

versions.name: GPT-5.6 Luna
versions.blurb: GPT-5.6 fast, cost-optimized tier, GA July 9, 2026, for high-volume batch and embedded work; 1.05M context; 82.5% on Terminal-Bench 2.1; $1/$6 per MTok.

versions.name: GPT-5.5
versions.blurb: Agentic flagship (April 2026); faster and more token-efficient than GPT-5.4, 1.05M context.

versions.name: GPT-5.4
versions.blurb: More affordable flagship; native Computer Use API, five-level reasoning effort, 1M+ context.

versions.name: GPT-5.2
versions.blurb: Knowledge-work upgrade in the GPT-5 series.

versions.name: GPT-5.1
versions.blurb: Warmer, more conversational and customizable GPT-5.

versions.name: GPT-5
versions.blurb: First GPT-5 generation.

versions.name: GPT-4o
versions.blurb: Prior multimodal flagship.

lines.title: GPT Pro
lines.blurb: OpenAI's highest-capability reasoning tier — uses extra test-time compute to think harder on research-grade problems.

versions.name: GPT-5.5 Pro
versions.blurb: Highest-reasoning tier; reasoning tokens, requests can take minutes.

versions.name: GPT-5.4 Pro
versions.blurb: Pro reasoning tier in the GPT-5.4 generation.

versions.name: GPT-5.2 Pro
versions.blurb: Extended-thinking Pro.

versions.name: GPT-5.1 Pro
versions.blurb: First 5.1-generation Pro reasoning model.

versions.name: GPT-5 Pro
versions.blurb: First GPT-5 Pro tier.

lines.title: GPT Instant
lines.blurb: OpenAI's fast, high-throughput default ChatGPT model line (API chat-latest) — low latency without extended reasoning.

versions.name: GPT-5.5 Instant
versions.blurb: New ChatGPT default; ~52% fewer hallucinations on high-stakes prompts vs GPT-5.3 Instant.

versions.name: GPT-5.3 Instant
versions.blurb: Smoother everyday conversations, better web search use.

versions.name: GPT-5.2 Instant
versions.blurb: GPT-5.2-generation fast model.

versions.name: GPT-5.1 Instant
versions.blurb: First model under the Instant branding.

lines.title: GPT Mini
lines.blurb: OpenAI's compact, lower-cost line for high-volume coding, tool use, and subagents.

versions.name: GPT-5.4 mini
versions.blurb: Strongest mini yet; 400K context, ~2x faster than GPT-5 mini, $0.75/$4.50 per 1M.

versions.name: GPT-5 mini
versions.blurb: First GPT-5-generation mini model.

versions.name: GPT-4o mini
versions.blurb: Prior-generation small multimodal model.

lines.title: GPT Nano
lines.blurb: OpenAI's smallest, cheapest API-only line for tasks where speed and cost matter most.

versions.name: GPT-5.4 nano
versions.blurb: Cheapest model in the family; 400K context, $0.20/$1.25 per 1M, API-only.

versions.name: GPT-5 nano
versions.blurb: First GPT-5-generation nano model.

lines.title: Codex
lines.blurb: OpenAI's coding-specialized model line, tuned for long-horizon agentic software engineering inside Codex.

versions.name: GPT-5.3-Codex
versions.blurb: Most capable agentic coding model; 400K context, 128K output, ~25% faster than 5.2-Codex.

versions.name: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark
versions.blurb: Smaller real-time research-preview variant; >1000 tok/s on Cerebras hardware.

versions.name: GPT-5.2-Codex
versions.blurb: Advanced agentic coding model for real-world software engineering.

versions.name: GPT-5.1-Codex
versions.blurb: Released with GPT-5.1-Codex-Max for long-horizon coding.

versions.name: GPT-5-Codex
versions.blurb: First Codex-branded GPT-5 coding model.

lines.title: gpt-oss (Open Weight)
lines.blurb: OpenAI's open-weight, Apache-2.0 Mixture-of-Experts models for local and on-device reasoning.

versions.name: gpt-oss-120b
versions.blurb: 117B total / 5.1B active MoE; 128K context, Apache-2.0, runs on a single 80GB GPU.

versions.name: gpt-oss-20b
versions.blurb: 21B total / 3.6B active MoE; 128K context, Apache-2.0, fits in ~16GB for on-device use.

lines.title: GPT Cyber
lines.blurb: Specialized, more permissive cybersecurity model for vetted defenders under OpenAI's Trusted Access for Cyber.

versions.name: GPT-5.5-Cyber
versions.blurb: Tuned to be more permissive on authorized security tasks (red teaming, pentest, malware study). Full release June 22, 2026.

lines.title: GPT-Rosalind
lines.blurb: OpenAI's domain-specific frontier reasoning model tuned for life sciences research, drug discovery, and genomics.

versions.name: GPT-Rosalind
versions.blurb: Frontier reasoning model for life sciences — drug discovery, genomics, medicinal chemistry; research preview via trusted-access since April 2026.

makers.title: Google
makers.blurb: Google ships its frontier LLMs under the Gemini brand, tiered into Pro (most capable), Flash (fast mid-tier), Flash-Lite (cheapest), and a Deep Think parallel-reasoning mode, alongside the open-weight Gemma family and an experimental text-diffusion line (Gemini Diffusion / DiffusionGemma).

lines.title: Gemini Pro
lines.blurb: Google's most capable flagship Gemini tier for complex reasoning, agentic, and coding tasks.

versions.name: Gemini 3.5 Pro
versions.blurb: Announced at Google I/O 2026 targeting June GA; as of mid-June 2026 not yet publicly shipped (internal/limited preview).

versions.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
versions.blurb: Gemini Pro flagship (Feb 2026); major reasoning gain (77.1% on ARC-AGI-2, more than double Gemini 3 Pro).

versions.name: Gemini 3 Pro
versions.blurb: First Gemini 3 flagship; sparse mixture-of-experts architecture.

versions.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
versions.blurb: Thinking model with chain-of-thought reasoning; later went GA.

versions.name: Gemini 2.0 Pro
versions.blurb: 2.0-generation Pro tier (experimental release).

versions.name: Gemini 1.5 Pro
versions.blurb: Introduced a very long context window (up to 1M tokens).

versions.name: Gemini 1.0 Ultra
versions.blurb: Most capable model of the original 1.0 family.

versions.name: Gemini 1.0 Pro
versions.blurb: Original mid-tier Gemini model.

lines.title: Gemini Flash
lines.blurb: Fast, cost-efficient mid-tier Gemini model balancing speed and intelligence.

versions.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
versions.blurb: Flash tier (May 2026); frontier performance for agents/coding, ~4x faster than other frontier models.

versions.name: Gemini 3 Flash
versions.blurb: Pro-grade reasoning at Flash latency/cost; ~3x faster than 2.5 Pro, 78% on SWE-bench Verified.

versions.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash
versions.blurb: First Flash model with thinking capability; updated 09-2025 preview improved agentic benchmarks.

versions.name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
versions.blurb: Multimodal, speed-focused 2.0 Flash (shut down June 1, 2026).

versions.name: Gemini 1.5 Flash
versions.blurb: Lightweight, fast 1.5-generation model with free tier.

lines.title: Gemini Flash-Lite
lines.blurb: Google's fastest and lowest-cost Gemini tier, optimized for intelligence per dollar.

versions.name: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite
versions.blurb: Flash-Lite tier; preview March 2026, GA May 7, 2026.

versions.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite
versions.blurb: Fastest/cheapest 2.5 model with optional toggleable reasoning; later GA. Updated 09-2025 preview cut output tokens ~50%.

versions.name: Gemini 2.0 Flash-Lite
versions.blurb: Cost-efficiency-optimized 2.0 tier (shut down June 1, 2026).

versions.name: Gemini 1.5 Flash-8B
versions.blurb: Smaller, ~50% cheaper 8B precursor to the Flash-Lite tier.

lines.title: Gemini Deep Think
lines.blurb: Extended parallel-reasoning mode that explores multiple ideas before answering, offered to top-tier (Ultra) subscribers.

versions.name: Gemini 3 Deep Think
versions.blurb: Deep Think reasoning mode (Dec 2025); rolled out to AI Ultra subscribers.

versions.name: Gemini 2.5 Deep Think
versions.blurb: Parallel-thinking reasoning mode for AI Ultra; IMO-level math variant.

lines.title: Gemma (open weights)
lines.blurb: Google's family of open-weight models derived from Gemini research, for local and commercial use.

versions.name: Gemma 4 12B
versions.blurb: Encoder-free unified multimodal Gemma (June 2026); ~12B dense, 256K context, native text/image/audio/video input on 16GB VRAM, Apache 2.0.

versions.name: Gemma 4
versions.blurb: Open Gemma generation (Apr 2026); first under Apache 2.0; sizes E2B/E4B/26B MoE/31B Dense, multimodal with audio on edge models.

versions.name: Gemma 3n
versions.blurb: Mobile-first efficient variant (E2B, E4B) for on-device use.

versions.name: Gemma 3
versions.blurb: 1B/4B/12B/27B sizes; multimodal (image), 128K context, 140+ languages; a 270M size added Aug 2025.

versions.name: Gemma 2
versions.blurb: 2B/9B/27B sizes using grouped-query attention.

versions.name: Gemma 1
versions.blurb: First open Gemma; 2B and 7B sizes, lightweight cousin of Gemini.

lines.title: Gemini Diffusion / DiffusionGemma
lines.blurb: Experimental text-diffusion line that generates text by denoising blocks in parallel for much higher throughput.

versions.name: DiffusionGemma
versions.blurb: First open-weight (Apache 2.0) text-diffusion model; 26B MoE (~3.8B active), up to 4x faster generation; experimental, lower quality than Gemma 4.

versions.name: Gemini Diffusion
versions.blurb: DeepMind research demo: first large-scale text-diffusion model, ~1,479 tokens/sec, waitlist-only.

lines.title: Gemini Nano
lines.blurb: On-device Gemini tier designed to run locally on phones and edge hardware.

versions.name: Gemini 1.0 Nano
versions.blurb: On-device model; first shipped in the Pixel 8 Pro.

lines.title: Med-Gemini / Med-PaLM (medical)
lines.blurb: Google's medical-domain large language models: Med-PaLM 2, which powers the MedLM API on Vertex AI, and the multimodal Med-Gemini research model.

versions.name: Med-PaLM 2
versions.blurb: Medical LLM that reached expert-level USMLE-style question answering (86.5% on MedQA); powers MedLM, offered to allowlisted Google Cloud customers via Vertex AI.

versions.name: Med-Gemini
versions.blurb: Multimodal medical research model built on Gemini; reported 91.1% on MedQA (USMLE) with an uncertainty-guided search strategy and new state of the art on 10 of 14 medical benchmarks.

makers.title: Meta
makers.blurb: Meta ships the open-weight Llama family (the flagship Llama 4 MoE "herd" plus the dense Llama 3.x, Llama 2 and Llama 1 generations), specialized lines (Code Llama for coding, Llama Guard and Prompt Guard for safety), and — since April 2026 — Muse Spark, the first proprietary, closed-weight frontier model from Meta Superintelligence Labs.

lines.title: Muse Spark
lines.blurb: Meta Superintelligence Labs' first proprietary, closed-weight frontier reasoning model, built on a new stack and served on Meta AI.

versions.name: Muse Spark 1.1
versions.blurb: Agentic upgrade to Muse Spark, released July 2026: 1M-token context with active compaction, multi-agent orchestration, computer use, and Meta's first paid API at $1.25/$4.25 per MTok.

versions.name: Muse Spark
versions.blurb: First model in the Muse family from Meta Superintelligence Labs, released April 2026: natively multimodal reasoning, tool use, visual chain-of-thought and multi-agent orchestration.

lines.title: Llama 4
lines.blurb: Meta's flagship open-weight, natively multimodal mixture-of-experts 'herd' — the Llama 4 generation.

versions.name: Llama 4 Maverick
versions.blurb: 17B active parameters, 128 experts, 400B total; natively multimodal (text + image in, text out), multilingual, ~1M-token context. Positioned as the h…

versions.name: Llama 4 Scout
versions.blurb: 17B active parameters, 16 experts, 109B total; natively multimodal MoE with an industry-leading 10M-token context window, runnable on a single H100 (…

versions.name: Llama 4 Behemoth
versions.blurb: Announced as a 288B-active-parameter, 16-expert (~2T total) teacher model used to distill Scout and Maverick; described as still in training and NOT…

lines.title: Llama 3
lines.blurb: The previous-generation open-weight dense (text and vision) Llama line, spanning the 3.0 through 3.3 point releases.

versions.name: Llama 3.3 70B
versions.blurb: 70B instruction-tuned text model delivering quality comparable to Llama 3.1 405B / 3.2 90B at much lower cost, especially on instruction following.

versions.name: Llama 3.2
versions.blurb: Added lightweight edge/mobile text models (1B, 3B) plus Meta's first Llama vision models (11B and 90B multimodal image-reasoning variants).

versions.name: Llama 3.1
versions.blurb: 8B, 70B and the frontier-scale 405B; 128K context and eight-language support — the first open frontier-level Llama model.

versions.name: Llama 3
versions.blurb: Initial Llama 3 release in 8B and 70B dense text models, pretrained on a much larger token budget than Llama 2.

lines.title: Llama 2
lines.blurb: Meta's second-generation open-weight dense LLM line, the first Llama released for broad commercial use.

versions.name: Llama 2
versions.blurb: Open-weight dense text models at 7B, 13B and 70B, released with a license permitting commercial use.

lines.title: Llama 1
lines.blurb: The original LLaMA research models that started the family.

versions.name: LLaMA
versions.blurb: First-generation LLaMA dense models (roughly 7B, 13B, 33B and 65B), released as research-only weights.

lines.title: Code Llama
lines.blurb: Specialized open-weight coding models fine-tuned from Llama 2 for code generation and understanding.

versions.name: Code Llama 70B
versions.blurb: Largest Code Llama model, trained on roughly 1T tokens of code; added to the existing 7B/13B/34B sizes.

versions.name: Code Llama
versions.blurb: Initial 7B, 13B and 34B code-specialized models built on Llama 2, each trained on ~500B tokens of code and code-related data.

makers.title: DeepSeek
makers.blurb: DeepSeek is a Chinese AI lab known for open-weight, cost-efficient large language models. Its lineup spans the V-series general MoE flagships (now V4-Pro / V4-Flash), the R1 reasoning line and its small distilled variants, and specialised open models for code, math, formal proving, vision-language, unified multimodal generation, and OCR.

lines.title: DeepSeek V4
lines.blurb: Flagship Mixture-of-Experts text model line with a native 1M-token context, shipped in a larger Pro tier and a faster, cheaper Flash tier.

versions.name: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
versions.blurb: Larger V4 tier: 1.6T total / 49B active MoE, 1M-token context, MIT-licensed open weights; built for coding and agentic tasks, with a V4-Pro-Max maxim…

versions.name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash
versions.blurb: Smaller, faster V4 tier: 284B total / 13B active MoE, 1M-token context, MIT-licensed. Cheapest tier at $0.14/M input (miss) / $0.28/M output; legacy…

lines.title: DeepSeek V3
lines.blurb: General-purpose open-weight MoE line (671B/685B total, ~37B active) that introduced sparse-attention efficiency and hybrid thinking/non-thinking modes; predecessor to V4.

versions.name: DeepSeek-V3.2
versions.blurb: Official successor to V3.2-Exp; bakes tool-use into both thinking and non-thinking modes, trained across 1,800+ environments. 685B-param open weights…

versions.name: DeepSeek-V3.2-Speciale
versions.blurb: Deep-reasoning-only variant of V3.2 (no tool calls); higher reasoning ceiling, released alongside V3.2 open weights.

versions.name: DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp
versions.blurb: Experimental release introducing DeepSeek Sparse Attention for long-context efficiency; set as the default chat/reasoner endpoint.

versions.name: DeepSeek-V3.1-Terminus
versions.blurb: V3.1 update reducing Chinese-English language mixing and improving agent/tool performance.

versions.name: DeepSeek-V3.1
versions.blurb: First hybrid model combining thinking and non-thinking modes in one checkpoint, with improved reasoning efficiency.

versions.name: DeepSeek-V3-0324
versions.blurb: V3 refresh with stronger reasoning and code generation (MMLU-Pro 75.9 to 81.2).

versions.name: DeepSeek-V3
versions.blurb: Original 671B-total / 37B-active MoE flagship trained on ~14.8T tokens; open weights.

versions.name: DeepSeek-V2.5
versions.blurb: Merged the V2 chat and coder models into one general assistant; revised as V2.5-1210 in December 2024.

versions.name: DeepSeek-V2
versions.blurb: First DeepSeek MoE flagship (236B total / 21B active) introducing Multi-head Latent Attention; open weights.

lines.title: DeepSeek R1
lines.blurb: First-generation reasoning line built on the V3 backbone with large-scale reinforcement learning, exposing chain-of-thought; open-weight under MIT.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-0528
versions.blurb: Major R1 upgrade with deeper reasoning and fewer hallucinations (AIME 70.0 to 87.5).

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1
versions.blurb: First-gen reasoning model reaching performance comparable to OpenAI o1 on math, code and reasoning; open weights.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-Zero
versions.blurb: RL-only variant trained without supervised fine-tuning, demonstrating emergent reasoning.

lines.title: DeepSeek R1 Distill
lines.blurb: Small dense models (1.5B-70B) fine-tuned on R1 reasoning traces over Qwen and Llama bases, bringing R1-style reasoning to commodity hardware.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B
versions.blurb: 8B distill of the updated R1-0528 onto a Qwen3 base.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B
versions.blurb: Largest original R1 distill, on a Llama 3.3 70B base.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B
versions.blurb: 32B Qwen 2.5 distill of R1 reasoning.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B
versions.blurb: 14B Qwen 2.5 distill of R1 reasoning.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B
versions.blurb: 8B Llama 3.1 distill of R1 reasoning.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B
versions.blurb: 7B Qwen 2.5 distill of R1 reasoning.

versions.name: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.5B
versions.blurb: Smallest R1 distill, on a 1.5B Qwen 2.5 base.

lines.title: DeepSeek Coder
lines.blurb: Code-specialised open-weight line; the original dense models plus the V2 MoE that reached GPT-4-Turbo-level code performance across 338 languages.

versions.name: DeepSeek-Coder-V2
versions.blurb: Open MoE code model (16B and 236B sizes, 2.4B/21B active) further pre-trained from V2; 338 languages and 128K context, GPT-4-Turbo-class on code.

versions.name: DeepSeek Coder
versions.blurb: DeepSeek's first model: dense code LLMs (1.3B-33B) trained on 2T code-heavy tokens.

lines.title: DeepSeek Math
lines.blurb: Math-reasoning line; the latest V2 trains a verifier-guided self-checking prover that reaches IMO/CMO gold level.

versions.name: DeepSeek-Math-V2
versions.blurb: 685B model built on V3.2-Exp-Base using verifier-rewarded self-verification; IMO 2025 and CMO 2024 gold-level, Putnam 2024 118/120. Apache-2.0.

versions.name: DeepSeekMath
versions.blurb: Original 7B math model that introduced GRPO reinforcement learning for chain-of-thought math.

lines.title: DeepSeek Prover
lines.blurb: Formal theorem-proving line for the Lean proof assistant, using RL with subgoal decomposition.

versions.name: DeepSeek-Prover-V2
versions.blurb: Lean 4 theorem prover in 7B and 671B sizes; RL for subgoal decomposition, state-of-the-art on formal-proof benchmarks. Open weights.

versions.name: DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5
versions.blurb: Earlier Lean theorem-proving model combining proof-step and whole-proof generation.

lines.title: DeepSeek VL
lines.blurb: Vision-language line for multimodal understanding (image + document + chart reasoning); V2 moved to a MoE backbone.

versions.name: DeepSeek-VL2
versions.blurb: MoE vision-language models (tiny/small/base) for advanced multimodal and document/OCR understanding. Open weights.

versions.name: DeepSeek-VL
versions.blurb: First-generation dense vision-language models (1.3B and 7B) for real-world multimodal understanding.

lines.title: DeepSeek LLM
lines.blurb: The original general-purpose dense LLM line (pre-MoE), now legacy and superseded by the V-series.

versions.name: DeepSeek LLM
versions.blurb: DeepSeek's first general LLM: dense 7B and 67B Base and Chat models; foundation for the later V-series.

makers.title: Alibaba (Qwen)
makers.blurb: Alibaba's Qwen (Tongyi Qianwen) is one of the most prolific model families in the world, spanning a proprietary Max flagship, a broad open-weight family (Apache 2.0), and specialised coding, vision-language, omni-modal, reasoning and image lines.

lines.title: Qwen-Max
lines.blurb: Alibaba's proprietary, API-only flagship — its largest and most capable text/reasoning model, accessible through Alibaba Cloud Model Studio.

versions.name: Qwen3.7-Max
versions.blurb: Text-only reasoning agent flagship with a 1M-token context window and native extended-thinking mode, built for long-horizon agentic workloads; propri…

versions.name: Qwen3-Max
versions.blurb: First trillion-parameter Qwen flagship (over 1T params, ~36T training tokens); proprietary, API-only. A Qwen3-Max-Thinking reasoning variant followed.

versions.name: Qwen2.5-Max
versions.blurb: Large-scale MoE proprietary flagship of the 2.5 generation, available via API and Qwen Chat.

lines.title: Qwen-Plus (multimodal agent)
lines.blurb: Proprietary, API-only mid-tier/multimodal sibling to Max — cheaper, and from 3.7 onward a vision-capable agent model that reads screens and drives GUIs.

versions.name: Qwen3.7-Plus
versions.blurb: Multimodal agent model adding image/video understanding, deep reasoning, tool invocation and autonomous iteration on top of the 3.7 base; proprietary…

versions.name: Qwen3.6-Plus
versions.blurb: Proprietary 'real world agents' Plus model of the 3.6 generation with stronger reasoning and more reliable agentic behaviour.

versions.name: Qwen3.5-Plus
versions.blurb: Hosted, low-cost multimodal Plus model — 1M context, reads images and video, built for agents.

lines.title: Qwen (open-weight)
lines.blurb: The flagship open-weight (Apache 2.0) family spanning edge-sized dense models to large MoE models, covering general text, reasoning and agentic use.

versions.name: Qwen3.6
versions.blurb: Open-weight (Apache 2.0) generation; Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (35B total / 3B active MoE, agentic coding, thinking-context preservation) on 2026-04-16 and a Q…

versions.name: Qwen3.5
versions.blurb: Open-weight flagship Qwen3.5-397B-A17B (397B total / 17B active) using a hybrid linear-attention + sparse-MoE architecture, native vision-language; s…

versions.name: Qwen3 (2507 update)
versions.blurb: Refreshed Qwen3 checkpoints with separate Instruct/Thinking variants and extended context (up to ~1M tokens): Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct/Thinking-2507…

versions.name: Qwen3
versions.blurb: Hybrid 'think deeper, act faster' family with dense 0.6B/1.7B/4B/8B/14B/32B and MoE 30B-A3B/235B-A22B models, dual thinking/non-thinking modes; train…

versions.name: Qwen2.5
versions.blurb: Widely-adopted open generation: dense 0.5B/1.5B/3B/7B/14B/32B/72B with longer context and stronger coding/math than Qwen2.

versions.name: Qwen2
versions.blurb: Earlier open family with 0.5B/1.5B/7B/72B dense and a 57B-A14B MoE model.

lines.title: Qwen-Coder
lines.blurb: Open-weight code-specialised models tuned for agentic coding, repository-scale context, and tool use.

versions.name: Qwen3-Coder-Next
versions.blurb: Hybrid-attention MoE (80B total / 3B active, 512 experts) coding model built on Qwen3-Next, with 256K native context and 70.6% on SWE-bench Verified at sharply lower inference cost than dense models with much larger active footprints.

versions.name: Qwen3-Coder
versions.blurb: Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct (480B total / 35B active MoE) for agentic coding, matching frontier closed models on SWE-bench-style tasks; a smaller…

versions.name: Qwen2.5-Coder
versions.blurb: Code models from 0.5B to 32B; the 32B was a leading open coding model of its generation.

lines.title: Qwen-VL
lines.blurb: Vision-language models that read images, documents, charts and video and (from Qwen3-VL) act as visual agents.

versions.name: Qwen3-VL
versions.blurb: Vision-language family with Instruct and Thinking variants, starting with 235B-A22B; later sizes (30B-A3B, 32B, 8B, 4B, 2B) rolled out through Octobe…

versions.name: Qwen2.5-VL
versions.blurb: VL models at 3B/7B/32B/72B with stronger document parsing, grounding and long-video understanding.

versions.name: Qwen2-VL
versions.blurb: First Qwen2-generation VL models (2B/7B/72B) with dynamic-resolution vision and strong OCR.

lines.title: Qwen-Omni
lines.blurb: Natively end-to-end omni-modal models that take text, image, audio and video in and stream text plus natural speech out in real time.

versions.name: Qwen3-Omni
versions.blurb: Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B (30B total / 3B active MoE) with Instruct, Thinking and Captioner variants; open-source SOTA on most of 36 audio/AV benchmarks; Ap…

versions.name: Qwen2.5-Omni
versions.blurb: First Omni models (3B/7B) accepting text, image, audio and video and generating text and speech.

lines.title: Qwen3-Next
lines.blurb: An efficiency-focused architecture line — a sparse MoE design delivering near-flagship quality at a fraction of the active parameters and inference cost.

versions.name: Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B
versions.blurb: 80B-total / 3B-active MoE with hybrid attention, in Instruct and Thinking variants; designed for high throughput and long context; open-weight.

lines.title: QwQ / QVQ (reasoning preview)
lines.blurb: Standalone open reasoning models that pioneered Qwen's chain-of-thought 'thinking' approach before it merged into the main Qwen3 family.

versions.name: QwQ-32B
versions.blurb: 32B open reasoning model trained with RL, competitive with much larger reasoning models on math and code; Apache 2.0.

versions.name: QVQ-72B-Preview
versions.blurb: Experimental visual-reasoning model built on Qwen2-VL-72B, adding step-by-step reasoning over images.

makers.title: Moonshot AI (Kimi)
makers.blurb: Moonshot AI is a Beijing-based AI lab behind the Kimi assistant. Its flagship line is the multi-trillion-parameter Kimi K-series (K3, K2, K1), joined by a proprietary moonshot-v1 API generation and several smaller open vision, audio, and efficient-attention research models.

lines.title: Kimi K3
lines.blurb: Moonshot's 2.8-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts flagship built on Stable LatentMoE (~16 of 896 experts active per token) with Kimi Delta Attention, native text/vision/video input, and a 1M-token context.

versions.name: Kimi K3
versions.blurb: Moonshot's 2.8T-parameter MoE flagship (Stable LatentMoE, ~16 of 896 experts active) with native text/vision/video and a 1M-token context, released July 2026. Live via the Kimi API and app; full weights announced for release by 2026-07-27.

lines.title: Kimi K2
lines.blurb: Moonshot's flagship open-weight 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model line (32B active) for agentic chat and coding.

versions.name: Kimi K2.7-Code
versions.blurb: Coding-focused agentic model built on K2.6; cuts thinking-token usage ~30% while scoring higher on coding benchmarks. Open weights on Hugging Face, M…

versions.name: Kimi K2.6
versions.blurb: Natively multimodal flagship adding video input; expands Agent Swarm to 300 sub-agents. 1T MoE / 32B active, 256K context, open weights.

versions.name: Kimi K2.5
versions.blurb: Native multimodal (image) model introducing instant + thinking modes and Agent Swarm (up to 100 coordinated sub-agents); 256K context.

versions.name: Kimi K2-Instruct-0905
versions.blurb: Updated K2-Instruct: improved coding performance and context window raised from 128K to 256K tokens.

versions.name: Kimi K2
versions.blurb: Original 1T-parameter MoE (32B active) released open-weight as Kimi-K2-Base and Kimi-K2-Instruct under a Modified MIT license; strong coding/math ben…

lines.title: Kimi K2 Thinking
lines.blurb: Open-weight reasoning ('thinking agent') variant of K2 that interleaves chain-of-thought with native tool calls.

versions.name: Kimi K2 Thinking
versions.blurb: 1T MoE reasoning model with native INT4 quantization and 256K context; can run 200-300 sequential tool calls. Open weights, Modified MIT.

lines.title: Kimi K1
lines.blurb: Earlier multimodal reasoning model line that preceded the K2 family.

versions.name: Kimi K1.5
versions.blurb: Multimodal reasoning model Moonshot claimed matched OpenAI o1 on math, coding, and multimodal reasoning.

lines.title: Moonshot v1
lines.blurb: Proprietary API-only generation models offered in 8K/32K/128K context tiers plus vision-preview variants.

versions.name: Moonshot v1
versions.blurb: Closed API generation family: moonshot-v1-8k / 32k / 128k text models and matching -vision-preview multimodal variants on the Kimi platform.

lines.title: Kimi-VL
lines.blurb: Open-weight 16B-parameter MoE (3B active) vision-language model line for multimodal reasoning and long-context understanding.

versions.name: Kimi-VL-A3B-Thinking-2506
versions.blurb: Updated thinking VL model: higher multimodal-reasoning accuracy (e.g. MathVision 56.9), ~20% shorter reasoning, and 4x higher single-image resolution…

versions.name: Kimi-VL-A3B-Thinking
versions.blurb: Reasoning-tuned variant of the original 16B/A3B vision-language model.

versions.name: Kimi-VL-A3B-Instruct
versions.blurb: Original open-source 16B MoE (3B active) vision-language instruct model.

lines.title: Kimi Linear
lines.blurb: Open-weight 48B/A3B model showcasing the Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) hybrid linear-attention architecture for efficient long context.

versions.name: Kimi Linear (48B-A3B)
versions.blurb: Hybrid linear-attention model (48B total / 3B active) using Kimi Delta Attention; cuts KV cache up to 75% and reaches up to 6x decoding throughput at…

lines.title: Kimi-Dev
lines.blurb: Open-weight coding model line specialized for software-engineering tasks like bug fixing and patch generation.

versions.name: Kimi-Dev-72B
versions.blurb: 72B coding LLM (built on Qwen2.5-72B, RL-tuned) reaching 60.4% on SWE-bench Verified, a then-SOTA for open-source coding models. MIT license.

lines.title: Moonlight
lines.blurb: Open-weight 16B/A3B MoE research model line trained with the Muon optimizer.

versions.name: Moonlight-16B-A3B
versions.blurb: Open 16B-parameter MoE (3B active) available in base and Instruct variants on Hugging Face.

makers.title: Z.ai (Zhipu / GLM)
makers.blurb: Z.ai (formerly Zhipu AI), originating from Tsinghua University, develops the open-weight GLM family of large language models — spanning a flagship agentic-coding line, a mid-size Air line, a small Flash line, vision-language (GLM-V) models, and specialised OCR, speech, image and video models. Most GLM weights ship under permissive MIT/Apache licenses.

lines.title: GLM (flagship)
lines.blurb: Z.ai's frontier flagship large language model line, built for reasoning, agentic workflows and long-horizon coding, with open weights.

versions.name: GLM-5.2
versions.blurb: ~744B-parameter MoE (40B active) with a 1M-token context window — ~5x GLM-5.1's ~200K — focused on long-horizon agentic coding; open weights under MI…

versions.name: GLM-5.1
versions.blurb: 744B MoE (40B active), 200K context / up to 128K output; tuned for sustained autonomous execution (up to ~8 hours on one task) and engineering delive…

versions.name: GLM-5
versions.blurb: First GLM-5: ~744B-parameter MoE (~40B active) with DeepSeek Sparse Attention, 200K context / up to 128K output; shifts toward agentic engineering; M…

versions.name: GLM-4.7
versions.blurb: Open-sourced iteration with stronger coding, complex reasoning and more stable agentic / tool-use behaviour over long multi-step tasks.

versions.name: GLM-4.6
versions.blurb: Coding-focused flagship: 355B-class MoE (~357B params), context expanded from 128K to 200K; MIT license.

versions.name: GLM-4.5
versions.blurb: Native agentic LLM, 355B total / 32B active MoE, 128K context, MIT license; unified reasoning, coding and tool use.

lines.title: GLM Turbo
lines.blurb: API-served, throughput-optimised variants of the GLM-5 flagship for high-volume agent tasks.

versions.name: GLM-5V-Turbo
versions.blurb: Multimodal Turbo variant for vision-based coding and agent workflows.

versions.name: GLM-5-Turbo
versions.blurb: High-throughput GLM-5 variant optimised for agent tasks with improved tool integration and instruction decomposition.

lines.title: GLM Air
lines.blurb: Mid-size, more compact open-weight GLM variant balancing capability and efficiency.

versions.name: GLM-4.5-Air
versions.blurb: Compact sibling of GLM-4.5: 106B total / 12B active MoE, 128K context, MIT license; near-flagship quality at much lower cost.

lines.title: GLM Flash
lines.blurb: Lightweight, low-latency free-tier GLM models for fast everyday tasks.

versions.name: GLM-4.7-Flash
versions.blurb: Lightweight free-tier model with competitive coding capability and low latency; open weights on Hugging Face.

lines.title: GLM-V (vision-language)
lines.blurb: Open-weight vision-language models for multimodal reasoning, GUI agents and document/image understanding.

versions.name: GLM-4.6V
versions.blurb: 106B vision-language model with native multimodal function calling (images/screenshots as tool inputs) and 128K context; MIT license. A 9B GLM-4.6V-F…

versions.name: GLM-4.5V
versions.blurb: 106B (12B active) open-source vision-reasoning model with thinking mode, built on the GLM-4.5-Air base; SoTA across many open-model vision benchmarks.

versions.name: GLM-4.1V-9B-Thinking
versions.blurb: 9B reasoning-focused vision-language model that outperforms much larger models on many benchmarks; the first GLM-V 'Thinking' release.

lines.title: GLM-4 (open 9B)
lines.blurb: The original small open-weight GLM-4 line — fully open models for local and research use.

versions.name: GLM-4-9B
versions.blurb: 9B open model (GLM-4-9B / GLM-4-9B-Chat) with tool calling and up to 128K context (1M-context and GLM-4V-9B variants also released); 26-language supp…

lines.title: AutoGLM
lines.blurb: Agentic models for autonomous device / phone and web automation.

versions.name: AutoGLM-Phone-Multilingual
versions.blurb: Mobile-automation agent supporting 50+ mainstream apps in English and Chinese.

makers.title: xAI (Grok)
makers.blurb: xAI is Elon Musk's AI lab. It ships the Grok family of large language models, with a flagship chat/reasoning line, faster low-cost variants, a max-reasoning multi-agent tier, dedicated coding models, and image/video generation models. Older Grok generations have been released as open weights.

lines.title: Grok (flagship)
lines.blurb: xAI's main general-purpose chat and reasoning model line.

versions.name: Grok 4.5
versions.blurb: SpaceXAI's July 2026 Grok flagship: Opus-class MoE trained jointly with Cursor; $2/$6 per 1M tokens; 83.3% Terminal-Bench 2.1, 64.7% SWE-Bench Pro.

versions.name: Grok 4.3
versions.blurb: Grok flagship released April 30, 2026; native video input, slide/document generation, December 2025 knowledge cutoff, and $1.25/$2.50 per 1M tokens with a 1M-context window.

versions.name: Grok 4.20
versions.blurb: Prior flagship; beta launched Feb 17, 2026, API variants (grok-4.20-0309 reasoning/non-reasoning/multi-agent) from March 10, 2026. Emphasized speed a…

versions.name: Grok 4.1
versions.blurb: Frontier conversational update: higher emotional intelligence, lower hallucination rate, stronger creative writing; topped LMArena Text Arena at laun…

versions.name: Grok 4
versions.blurb: Major reasoning release with native tool use and real-time (X) search integration; introduced the Grok 4 Heavy multi-agent tier alongside it.

versions.name: Grok 3
versions.blurb: Trained with ~10x more compute than Grok 2; added explicit reasoning/thinking modes. Shipped with a smaller Grok 3 mini.

versions.name: Grok 2
versions.blurb: Upgraded performance over Grok 1.5; added image generation (initially via Flux). Grok 2.5 weights were later open-sourced on Hugging Face (Aug 24, 20…

versions.name: Grok 1.5
versions.blurb: Improved reasoning and a 128K-token context window over Grok 1.

versions.name: Grok 1
versions.blurb: First Grok model; an early beta. Weights open-sourced under Apache 2.0 in March 2024.

lines.title: Grok Heavy
lines.blurb: Max-reasoning, multi-agent tier that runs several agents in parallel for the hardest problems.

versions.name: Grok 4 Heavy
versions.blurb: xAI's most powerful reasoning configuration; spawns multiple agents in parallel and compares results. Offered on the top SuperGrok Heavy subscription…

lines.title: Grok Fast
lines.blurb: Lower-cost, lower-latency variants of the flagship aimed at high-volume and agentic API use.

versions.name: Grok 4.1 Fast
versions.blurb: Optimized for tool-calling and agentic workflows; ~2M token context; shipped with the Agent Tools API. Very low API pricing with cheap cached input.

versions.name: Grok 4 Fast
versions.blurb: Faster, cheaper Grok 4 variant using ~40% fewer thinking tokens with comparable quality; up to 2M-token context.

lines.title: Grok Code
lines.blurb: Coding-specialised models tuned for fast, agentic software development.

versions.name: Grok Build 0.1
versions.blurb: Fast coding model trained specifically for agentic coding; 256K context, priced $1.00/$2.00 per M tokens in the xAI API.

versions.name: Grok Code Fast 1
versions.blurb: First dedicated agentic coding model (codename 'sonic'); 256K context, high throughput, low cost. Deprecated May 15, 2026 (retires Aug 15, 2026).

makers.title: Mistral AI
makers.blurb: Mistral AI is a Paris-based lab shipping a broad mix of open-weight and premier LLMs across flagship (Large), mid-tier (Medium), small/edge (Small, Ministral), reasoning (Magistral), coding (Codestral, Devstral), vision (Pixtral) and audio (Voxtral) lines, plus legacy Mixtral/Mistral-7B open models.

lines.title: Mistral Large
lines.blurb: Mistral's frontier flagship line for general-purpose chat, reasoning and coding.

versions.name: Mistral Large 3
versions.blurb: First MoE flagship since Mixtral; 41B active / 675B total params, 256k context, Apache 2.0 open weights.

versions.name: Mistral Large 2.1 (24.11)
versions.blurb: 123B dense refresh of Large 2 with better long-context, function calling and system-prompt handling (mistral-large-2411).

versions.name: Mistral Large 2 (24.07)
versions.blurb: 123B dense, 128k context, open weights; big jump in reasoning, math, code and multilingual (mistral-large-2407).

versions.name: Mistral Large (24.02)
versions.blurb: First-generation proprietary flagship, multilingual with coding (mistral-large-2402).

lines.title: Mistral Medium
lines.blurb: Mid-tier line balancing cost and capability for enterprise workloads.

versions.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
versions.blurb: 128B frontier-class multimodal model merging chat, reasoning and code; open weights (Modified MIT), 256k context, $1.5/$7.5 per Mtok.

versions.name: Mistral Medium 3.1
versions.blurb: Incremental update to Medium 3 (mistral-medium-2508).

versions.name: Mistral Medium 3
versions.blurb: Enterprise-focused mid-tier model; strong cost-to-performance (mistral-medium-2505).

versions.name: Mistral Medium (2023)
versions.blurb: Original proprietary mid-tier model offered via API.

lines.title: Mistral Small
lines.blurb: Compact open-weight line for low-latency, locally deployable general use.

versions.name: Mistral Small 4
versions.blurb: 119B-total / 6B-active MoE unifying reasoning, vision and coding with a per-request reasoning_effort control; Apache 2.0, 256k context (mistral-small…

versions.name: Mistral Small 3.2
versions.blurb: 24B refresh improving instruction adherence and reliability; Apache 2.0.

versions.name: Mistral Small 3.1
versions.blurb: Adds multimodal (vision) and 128k context to the 24B Small line (mistral-small-2503).

versions.name: Mistral Small 3
versions.blurb: Latency-optimized 24B dense, Apache 2.0 (mistral-small-2501).

versions.name: Mistral Small (24.09)
versions.blurb: Earlier proprietary Small release (mistral-small-2409).

lines.title: Ministral
lines.blurb: Tiny dense edge models for on-device, laptop and drone deployment.

versions.name: Ministral 3 (3B / 8B / 14B)
versions.blurb: Three dense edge models with base/instruct/reasoning + vision variants, Apache 2.0 (ministral-3b/8b/14b-2512).

versions.name: Ministral 3B / 8B (24.10)
versions.blurb: First-generation edge models at 3B and 8B (ministral-3b/8b-2410).

lines.title: Magistral
lines.blurb: Chain-of-thought reasoning line in open Small and premier Medium sizes.

versions.name: Magistral 1.2 (Small & Medium)
versions.blurb: Adds image understanding; Small 1.2 fits on a laptop (magistral-small/medium-2509).

versions.name: Magistral 1.1 (Small & Medium)
versions.blurb: Second iteration of the reasoning models.

versions.name: Magistral 1.0 (Small & Medium)
versions.blurb: Mistral's first reasoning models; Small (24B, Apache 2.0) and premier Medium (magistral-*-2506).

lines.title: Codestral
lines.blurb: Code-completion / fill-in-the-middle line covering 80+ languages.

versions.name: Codestral (25.08)
versions.blurb: Code-completion model (codestral-2508, July 2025).

versions.name: Codestral 25.01
versions.blurb: ~2x faster generation, 80+ languages, 256k context (codestral-2501).

versions.name: Codestral 22B (24.05)
versions.blurb: Original 22B code model (codestral-2405).

lines.title: Devstral
lines.blurb: Agentic coding line built for end-to-end software-engineering tasks (SWE-bench).

versions.name: Devstral 2 / Devstral Small 2
versions.blurb: 123B (Modified MIT, 72.2% SWE-bench) plus 24B Small 2 (Apache 2.0, 68.0%); both 256k context (devstral-2512 / labs-devstral-small-2512).
versions.license: Devstral 2: Modified MIT; Devstral Small 2: Apache 2.0

versions.name: Devstral Medium & Small 1.1 (25.07)
versions.blurb: Adds a premier Devstral Medium and an updated Small 1.1 (devstral-medium/small-2507).
versions.license: Devstral Small 1.1: Apache 2.0; Devstral Medium: proprietary (API only)

versions.name: Devstral Small (25.05)
versions.blurb: First Devstral; 24B agentic coder built with All Hands AI, Apache 2.0, runs on a single RTX 4090 (devstral-small-2505).

lines.title: Pixtral
lines.blurb: Multimodal vision-language line (now largely folded into newer unified models).

versions.name: Pixtral Large (24.11)
versions.blurb: 124B vision model built on Mistral Large 2, 128k context, open weights (pixtral-large-2411).

versions.name: Pixtral 12B (24.09)
versions.blurb: First Mistral vision model, 12B with image understanding (pixtral-12b-2409).

lines.title: Mixtral
lines.blurb: Legacy open-weight sparse mixture-of-experts line that put Mistral on the map.

versions.name: Mixtral 8x22B
versions.blurb: 141B-total sparse MoE, Apache 2.0.

versions.name: Mixtral 8x7B
versions.blurb: First Mistral MoE; 46.7B total params, Apache 2.0.

lines.title: Mistral 7B / Nemo (open dense)
lines.blurb: Legacy open-weight dense base models.

versions.name: Mistral NeMo (12B)
versions.blurb: 12B dense model built with NVIDIA, 128k context, Apache 2.0 (open-mistral-nemo).

versions.name: Mistral 7B
versions.blurb: Mistral's debut open model; 7.3B params, Apache 2.0.

lines.title: Leanstral
lines.blurb: Mistral's open-weight code agent line tuned for Lean 4 proof engineering and formal code verification.

versions.name: Leanstral 1.5
versions.blurb: Apache-2.0 MoE (119B total / 6.5B active) for Lean 4 proof engineering (July 2026); saturates miniF2F, solves 587/672 on PutnamBench, 256K context.

lines.title: Specialist open models (Mathstral / Codestral Mamba)
lines.blurb: One-off open-weight research models for math and efficient long-context code.

versions.name: Mathstral 7B
versions.blurb: 7B model specialised for STEM/math reasoning, Apache 2.0.

versions.name: Codestral Mamba
versions.blurb: Mamba-architecture code model for efficient long-context inference (open-codestral-mamba).

makers.title: Cohere
makers.blurb: Cohere is an enterprise-focused AI lab whose Command family of large language models targets retrieval-augmented generation, tool use, agents, and multilingual business workloads. Its research arm, Cohere Labs, ships the open-weight Aya family of multilingual models and the North family of agentic coding models.

lines.title: Command A+
lines.blurb: Cohere's flagship Mixture-of-Experts model unifying reasoning, vision, and agentic tool use.

versions.name: Command A+
versions.blurb: First Apache-2.0 Command frontier model; sparse MoE (218B total / 25B active), 128K context, vision + reasoning + tool use, 48 languages, runs on 1x…

lines.title: Command A
lines.blurb: Efficient enterprise workhorse for RAG, agents, and multilingual tool use.

versions.name: Command A (03-2025)
versions.blurb: 256K context, runs on two GPUs with ~150% the throughput of Command R+ 08-2024; strong on tool use, RAG, and agents across 23 languages.

lines.title: Command A Reasoning
lines.blurb: Cohere's reasoning-tuned model with a token-budgeted thinking phase for agents.

versions.name: Command A Reasoning (08-2025)
versions.blurb: Cohere's first reasoning model; controllable token-budgeted thinking, 256K context, 23 languages, built for enterprise agents, tool use, and multi-st…

lines.title: Command A Vision
lines.blurb: Multimodal Command model for charts, tables, and document understanding.

versions.name: Command A Vision (07-2025)
versions.blurb: Cohere's first commercial vision model; 128K context, handles chart analysis, table parsing, document OCR, and multilingual image-text recognition.

lines.title: Command A Translate
lines.blurb: Dedicated enterprise translation model for 23 business languages.

versions.name: Command A Translate (08-2025)
versions.blurb: Translation-specialized 111B model across 23 languages, 16K total context (8K in / 8K out), runs on one or two GPUs; secure private deployment option.

lines.title: North Mini Code
lines.blurb: Cohere Labs' open coding sub-agent line: Apache-2.0 sparse-MoE models tuned for agentic software engineering.

versions.name: North Mini Code 1.0
versions.blurb: First North-family model; 30B / 3B-active sparse MoE under Apache 2.0, 256K context, 67.6% SWE-Bench Verified, single-H100 deployment at FP8.

lines.title: Command R
lines.blurb: Scalable R-series model optimized for RAG, tool use, and long context.

versions.name: Command R (08-2024)
versions.blurb: August refresh: ~50% higher throughput, 20-25% lower latency, improved multilingual RAG/tool use and math/code/reasoning, added Safety Modes; 128K co…

versions.name: Command R (03-2024)
versions.blurb: Original Command R: 128K context, RAG and tool-use focus, 10 primary languages; deprecated Sept 15, 2025.

lines.title: Command R+
lines.blurb: The larger, higher-accuracy R-series model for demanding enterprise workflows.

versions.name: Command R+ (08-2024)
versions.blurb: August refresh of the flagship R+ with improved performance and lower price ($2.50/M input); 128K context, 10+ languages.
versions.license: Proprietary (API); open weights under CC-BY-NC on Hugging Face

versions.name: Command R+ (04-2024)
versions.blurb: Original Command R+: most powerful Cohere LLM at launch, 128K context, 10+ languages, advanced multi-step tool use; deprecated Sept 15, 2025.
versions.license: Proprietary (API); open weights under CC-BY-NC on Hugging Face

lines.title: Command R7B
lines.blurb: Smallest, fastest R-series model for speed- and cost-sensitive deployments.

versions.name: Command R7B (12-2024)
versions.blurb: 7B-class smallest R-series model with 128K context; tuned for low latency and cost, RAG and tool use; open weights on Hugging Face.

lines.title: Command (legacy)
lines.blurb: Cohere's first-generation Command generation models, now retired.

versions.name: Command / Command Light
versions.blurb: Original 4K-context Command and lighter Command Light generation models; both deprecated Sept 15, 2025 in favor of Command R / Command A.

lines.title: Aya Expanse
lines.blurb: Open-weight multilingual model family from Cohere Labs covering 23 languages.

versions.name: Aya Expanse (8B & 32B)
versions.blurb: Open-weight multilingual models in 8B and 32B sizes across 23 languages; 32B remains live, 8B retired Apr 4, 2026.

lines.title: Aya Vision
lines.blurb: Open-weight multilingual multimodal model from Cohere Labs.

versions.name: Aya Vision (8B & 32B)
versions.blurb: Cohere Labs' first open-weight vision model in 8B and 32B sizes; multilingual captioning, VQA, and translation across 23 languages (non-commercial CC…
versions.license: CC-BY-NC 4.0 (with Cohere Lab's Acceptable Use Policy)

lines.title: Tiny Aya
lines.blurb: Tiny open-weight multilingual models from Cohere Labs for on-device use.

versions.name: Tiny Aya
versions.blurb: Open-weight 3.35B multilingual research models (global/earth/fire/water variants) covering 70+ languages, optimized for efficient local deployment un…

makers.title: MiniMax
makers.blurb: MiniMax is a Shanghai-based AI lab whose LLM portfolio spans the flagship M-series agentic/coding models (M2 through M3), the M1 open-weight reasoning model, the MiniMax-01 lightning-attention foundation line, and the legacy abab series.

lines.title: MiniMax M-Series
lines.blurb: MiniMax's flagship MoE LLM line built for coding and agentic/tool-use workflows, shipped as fast point releases.

versions.name: MiniMax M3
versions.blurb: New MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) architecture; 1M-token context, native multimodal input, open-weight; per-token compute ~1/20 of prior gen; 59.0%…

versions.name: MiniMax M2.7 / M2.7-highspeed
versions.blurb: Self-improvement-focused update; ~205K context; standard plus a low-latency highspeed variant; GPQA Diamond 87.4%.

versions.name: MiniMax M2.5 / M2.5-Lightning
versions.blurb: 230B-total / 10B-active MoE, ~205K context; 80.2% SWE-Bench Verified; standard (50 tok/s) plus faster Lightning (100 tok/s) variant.

versions.name: MiniMax M2.1
versions.blurb: Large multilingual-coding gains (Rust, Java, Go, C++, Kotlin, etc.); SWE-bench Multilingual 56.5%->72.5%; 230B/10B MoE.

versions.name: MiniMax M2
versions.blurb: Open-source (MIT) 230B-total / 10B-active MoE for coding and agentic tool calling; 69.4 SWE-bench Verified; ~8% of Claude Sonnet price.

lines.title: MiniMax M1
lines.blurb: Open-weight hybrid-attention reasoning model with large test-time compute and 1M-token context.

versions.name: MiniMax-M1 (M1-40k / M1-80k)
versions.blurb: World's first open-weight large-scale hybrid-attention (MoE + lightning attention) reasoning model; 456B-total / 45.9B-active, 1M context, Apache-2.0…

lines.title: MiniMax-01 (Text-01)
lines.blurb: Foundation LLM line pioneering lightning (linear) attention at scale for extreme context lengths.

versions.name: MiniMax-Text-01
versions.blurb: Open-source 456B-total / 45.9B-active hybrid MoE with lightning + softmax attention; 1M-token training context, extrapolates to 4M at inference.

lines.title: abab (legacy)
lines.blurb: MiniMax's earlier proprietary general LLM series, since superseded by the M-series and MiniMax-01.

versions.name: abab6.5 / abab6.5s
versions.blurb: Trillion-parameter MoE with 200K-token context; abab6.5s is a more efficient variant (same 200K context, ~30k words/sec).

versions.name: abab6
versions.blurb: First 100B+-parameter MoE-architecture LLM in China; predecessor to the abab6.5 series.

versions.name: abab5.5 / abab5.5s
versions.blurb: Earlier general-purpose abab models (abab5.5 ~16K-token I/O limit; abab5.5s a smaller character/persona variant); abab line opened publicly Aug 2023.

makers.title: ByteDance
makers.blurb: ByteDance's Seed team develops proprietary large language models under the Doubao/Seed brand, delivered through Volcano Engine and the Doubao assistant. Its Seed models target agentic productivity across coding, tool use and multimodal reasoning.

lines.title: Doubao Seed
lines.blurb: ByteDance's flagship general-purpose LLM family, positioned as an agent-first productivity model, delivered via the Doubao app and Volcano Engine API.

versions.name: Seed 2.1 Pro
versions.blurb: Doubao Seed 2.1 flagship (Volcano Engine FORCE, June 24, 2026): multimodal agent model with published benchmark leads on MathVision, VideoMME, WorldVQA and BabyVision.

makers.title: Tencent
makers.blurb: Tencent's Hy Team (formerly Hunyuan) develops the Hunyuan family of large language models, delivered as open weights on Hugging Face and served commercially through Tencent Cloud TokenHub and the Yuanbao assistant. Hy3, released July 6, 2026 under Apache 2.0, is the line's flagship agent-first Mixture-of-Experts model.

lines.title: Hunyuan
lines.blurb: Tencent's flagship open-weight LLM family. Hy3 is a 295B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with a switchable reasoning-effort mode, released July 6, 2026 under Apache 2.0.

versions.name: Hunyuan Hy3
versions.blurb: Tencent Hy Team's 295B / 21B-active MoE flagship, released July 6, 2026 under Apache 2.0 with a 256K context and a switchable no_think / low / high reasoning-effort mode.
